title: Loops with at most one iteration should be refactored
description: Loops that execute at most one iteration are misleading and should be refactored. Such loops are equivalent to conditional statements and make code harder to understand. If the intention is to execute code conditionally, use an if statement instead. If the loop should iterate multiple times, remove the premature break/return/throw statements.
recommendation: Replace single-iteration loops with if statements or fix the loop logic to allow multiple iterations.

noncompliant: for(let i = 0; i < 10; i++) { console.log(i); break; }
do { process(); throw new Error(); } while(condition);

checks.message: Loop executes at most once - consider using if statement or fix loop logic
